name: Open Terminal
description: Run shell commands, manage files and drive interactive processes on a remote machine via a self-hosted Open Terminal (open-webui/open-terminal) REST API. Works against a local box (via tunnel) or a VPS/Docker sandbox — same skill, only the URL differs.

credentials_required.label: Open Terminal Base URL
credentials_required.hint: Base URL of your Open Terminal instance, e.g. http://172.17.0.1:8000 (VPS Docker) or your public tunnel URL (local machine). No trailing slash needed.

credentials_required.label: Open Terminal API Key
credentials_required.hint: The OPEN_TERMINAL_API_KEY you set when starting Open Terminal (sent as Bearer token).

tools.name: run_command
tools.description: Execute a shell command and return stdout/stderr + exit code. Polls until the command finishes. Soft-warns on destructive commands (confirm=true to override).

tools.name: send_input
tools.description: Send text to the stdin of a still-running process (interactive prompts, sudo password, 2FA codes). Use the process_id from a previous run_command.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a text file's contents, optionally a line range.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write text content to a file (creates parent dirs, overwrites if it exists).

tools.name: list_files
tools.description: List files and directories at a given path.

tools.name: check_terminal
tools.description: Check that the Open Terminal instance is reachable and report enabled features.

author: freddy-schuetz
